message:
feat(core,abi): port GNU catgets binary catalog parser to core::locale::catgets (bd-cat-epic)

Lifts ~140 lines of pure binary-format parsing — parse_catalog_bytes,
MessageCatalog struct + message_ptr lookup, catalog_word helper, and
the 4 magic/layout constants — out of frankenlibc-abi/src/locale_abi.rs
into a pure-safe frankenlibc_core::locale::catgets module.

New core surface:
  pub const CATGETS_MAGIC: u32 = 0x9604_08de;
  pub const CATALOG_HEADER_WORDS: usize = 3;
  pub const CATALOG_SLOT_WORDS: usize = 3;
  pub enum CatalogParseError {
      InvalidMagic, TruncatedHeader, ZeroPlane,
      ArithmeticOverflow, TruncatedTable, MissingNul,
  }
  pub struct MessageCatalog { plane_size, plane_depth, table, strings }
  pub fn parse_catalog_bytes(bytes) -> Result<MessageCatalog, CatalogParseError>
  impl MessageCatalog {
      pub fn message_offset(&self, set_id, msg_id) -> Option<usize>;
      pub fn message_bytes(&self, set_id, msg_id) -> Option<&[u8]>;
  }

The parser:
  - Bidirectionally detects the catalog magic (LE / BE-swapped header)
  - Uses checked_mul / checked_add for the plane footprint computation
    (rejects ArithmeticOverflow rather than wrapping)
  - Reads the first hash plane as little-endian (matching glibc — the
    second-plane endianness is not renegotiated by a swapped header)
  - Validates that the largest referenced string offset is in-range
    AND that a NUL terminator exists past it
  - Returns typed errors instead of `c_int` errno values

abi changes:
  - parse_catalog_bytes shrinks to a 1-line shim that maps
    CatalogParseError → libc::EINVAL, preserving the previous errno.
  - catgets calls catalog.message_offset() and constructs the
    *const c_char from the strings-blob slice at the boundary —
    keeping the unsafe pointer construction out of core.
  - The CatalogRegistry / next_catalog_id / nl_catd descriptor
    allocator stay at the abi boundary where they have to be.
  - Pre-existing let-Some-else syntax errors in wchar_abi.rs (from a
    prior linter pass) corrected to compile under the test profile.

Verified via rch (CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc_bd-cat-2):
  - 14 new core::locale::catgets unit tests pass
  - 173/173 abi lib tests pass
  - 50/50 locale_abi integration tests pass
  - cargo clippy -p core -p abi -D warnings clean
  - cargo fmt --check clean

Closes bd-cat-epic.
